behaviors.description: Traveling-salesman heuristic route length is <= 1.5x optimal for up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is about the heuristic algorithm’s output quality versus an optimal solver, which is a single-function/algorithm boundary. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process generated coordinates are sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking dependencies does not hide the core failure because the failure is in route-construction logic itself. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: System/Agentic/Workflow were rejected because behavior text contains no real infra or AI dependency; Integration was rejected because no cross-module handoff is required to trigger this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic coordinate sets (sizes 5..20), compute heuristic route length and exact optimal length (e.g., Held-Karp/brute force for bounded n), assert ratio <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: Delivery time windows are computed from inter-stop distance and configured road-type average speed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The behavior targets deterministic calculation logic (distance/speed -> time window). REAL-INFRA: No real HTTP/OS/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: Mocks do not inherently hide failures in the formula or unit conversion. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text does not require proving module handoff, only correctness of computation from given inputs/config.
behaviors.test_description: Call the estimator with fixed leg distances and road-type speeds; assert exact/within-tolerance time window outputs, including mixed road types and conversion edge cases.

behaviors.description: Shift-hours validator rejects routes exceeding remaining shift time and returns excess duration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is a pure validation rule on totals and remaining hours. REAL-INFRA: No real infrastructure needed. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking would not mask the boundary condition bug because the failure is arithmetic/threshold logic. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is a single validator decision with deterministic inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with totals just below, equal to, and above remaining shift hours; assert accept/reject and exact excess amount for reject cases.

behaviors.description: If one address fails geocoding (no results), service skips it, optimizes remaining addresses, and reports skipped addresses.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure appears at module handoff (geocoder result handling -> stop filtering -> optimizer input -> reporting), not a single function. REAL-INFRA: A stubbed geocoder response can reproduce 'no results'; real network is not required. MOCK-HIDE: Pure unit tests of each module can all pass while integration still drops wrong stop/report. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No AI model dependency or multi-agent chain. AGENT-MEMORY-SLOTS: Slot A='skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped'; Slot B='handoff'; Slot C='If lowered to Unit, you can miss mismatch where optimizer excludes the stop but response omits it from skipped list.' INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior explicitly concerns logical handling of a no-results API payload, reproducible with in-process stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with real skip/filter/report modules and a fake geocoder returning no-results for one address and coordinates for others; assert optimized route excludes failed address and skipped-address list contains exactly that address.

behaviors.description: Duplicate input addresses are consolidated into one stop with combined delivery items and not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The bug is typically at boundary between dedup/aggregation and route planning (state handoff of merged stop/items). REAL-INFRA: No real OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: Unit-testing only dedup logic may pass while planner still receives duplicates due to wiring/serialization mismatch. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No AI dependency. AGENT-MEMORY-SLOTS: Slot A='consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice'; Slot B='handoff'; Slot C='If lowered to Unit, you can miss case where items merge correctly but optimizer still visits duplicate coordinates as separate stops.' INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real infra need, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior does not require real external geocoding/network conditions; it is local module interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run service flow with duplicate addresses carrying different item payloads; assert one stop in optimizer input/output for that address and aggregated item list/quantity is preserved.
